chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.3599 of 2025 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz ul Hassan Shah. Mst. Nusrat..............Vs..................... The State 02.03.2026. Mr. Muhammad Shaharyar, Advocates for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Addl. P.G. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant is seeking post arrest bail FIR bearing Cr. No.1509/2025 U/s 9 3 (C), 3(A), CNS Act, 2024 of P.S. Korangi Industrial Area, Karachi. He was arrested by a police team of P.S. KIA on 06.12.2025 from near Jalal Chowk, Sector 6-G Mehran Town, Karachi and from her allegedly 1010 grams of Charas and 56 grams of Ice were recovered, hence she was booked in this case. 2. Applicant’s counsel has pleaded for bail on the ground that prior to this FIR, husband of applicant and her sister in law had moved applications against the complainant and FIRs were registered against him. As a backlash, he has got false FIR registered against the applicant lady. In order to prove this fact, he has filed relevant copies of FIRs against police officials. 3. Learned Addl.P.G. has, opposed bail citing criminal record of the applicant. However, learned counsel for applicant has submitted copies of judgments acquitting the applicant in all the cases. We, therefore, keeping in view the aforesaid fact and the material including FIRs registered against complainant by the family of the applicant, are of the view that the case against the applicant is one of further inquiry, hence allow this application and grant bail to the applicant subject to her furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court.

However, learned counsel for applicant has submitted copies of judgments acquitting the applicant in all the cases. We, therefore, keeping in view the aforesaid fact and the material including FIRs registered against complainant by the family of the applicant, are of the view that the case against the applicant is one of further inquiry, hence allow this application and grant bail to the applicant subject to her furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
